name: Grilled Zucchini & Mixed-Herb Marinade
subtitle: OLIVE OIL, LEMON JUICE, ORANGE JUICE, GARLIC, PARSLEY, THYME, CHERVIL
intro: THE COMBINATION of sautéed zucchini and tomatoes that’s traditionally eaten during Ramadan in Egypt inspired this dish. It’s a lovely side, and you could also eat it as a light meal by topping it with Eggplant, Tomatoes, and Peanuts, a recipe inspired by the Moroccan dish zaalouk.
method: Put the oil, lemon juice, orange juice, garlic, parsley, thyme, chervil, and salt in a large bowl and whisk to combine. Add the zucchini and toss until evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 3 hours or overnight, tossing occasionally.
Prepare a medium heat grill. Drain the zucchini, arrange on the grill grate, and grill until they begin to char, 4 to 6 minutes. Turn with tongs and cook for 4 to 6 more minutes.
Cut the zucchini into thirds crosswise, transfer to a bowl, sprinkle with pepper, and serve.
